name: Verify Out-of-Network Coverage Before a Planned Visit
description: Call a health insurer to pre-verify out-of-network (OON) coverage for a planned visit, procedure, or admission. Get the OON deductible, coinsurance, allowed-amount methodology, and any pre-auth or notification requirements — IN WRITING via a reference number that survives later disputes.
disclaimer: Agent must NEVER admit fault, NEVER agree to a settlement on the operator's behalf, NEVER speculate about prior conditions, NEVER sign a release. All of those route through ask_operator.

context.when_to_use: The user is planning to see a provider, get a procedure, or be admitted out-of-network and wants to know what the carrier will actually pay before incurring the cost. Use for elective visits, second opinions, specialist consults, and planned admissions.
preconditions: Member ID and group number ready.
Provider's name, NPI, tax ID, and address ready.
CPT/HCPCS code(s) for the planned service (the provider's billing office can supply).
User's OON benefit summary if available (PPO vs EPO/HMO — many plans have ZERO OON coverage except emergencies).

principles: OON benefits are calculated against an 'allowed amount' that is almost NEVER the provider's billed charge. Understanding the methodology (Medicare rate × N, UCR, Fair Health) is the entire game.

Balance billing for the difference between billed and allowed is legal in most non-emergency OON contexts. The No Surprises Act covers SOME scenarios (emergency, OON-at-INN-facility) but not planned OON visits.

Verbal verification is NOT binding on most plans. The phrase 'verification is not a guarantee of payment' is in nearly every member handbook — but a reference number creates a paper trail for disputes.
Ask for the allowed-amount METHODOLOGY, not just a number. 'Reasonable and customary' is a methodology category, not an answer.
Single-case agreements (SCAs) and gap exceptions exist — if no in-network provider has the right expertise, the carrier may agree to cover OON at INN rates.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'd like to verify out-of-network benefits for a planned [visit / procedure] on [date] with provider [name, NPI]. The CPT codes are [codes]. Could you give me a reference number for this verification call?
phrases.ask_oon_deductible_coinsurance: What's my out-of-network deductible — individual and family — and how much have I met year-to-date? After deductible, what's the OON coinsurance, and what's the OON out-of-pocket max?
phrases.ask_allowed_amount_methodology: What allowed-amount methodology does the plan use for OON claims? Medicare-based — and if so, what multiple? UCR? Fair Health? And what's the projected allowed amount for CPT [code]?
phrases.ask_balance_billing_exposure: If the provider's billed charge is higher than the allowed amount, am I balance-billed for the difference? Does the No Surprises Act apply to this scenario?
phrases.ask_preauth_requirements: Does this service require prior authorization, advance notification, or any other paperwork before the visit? Even though it's out of network?
phrases.ask_single_case_agreement: Is there a process for requesting a single-case agreement or gap exception so this visit is covered at in-network rates? What's the criteria and submission path?
phrases.request_written_summary: Could you email or mail a written summary of this verification — reference number, OON benefits, allowed-amount methodology, and any pre-auth requirements?
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. To confirm: reference number [N], OON deductible remaining [amount], OON coinsurance [%], OOP max remaining [amount], allowed-amount methodology [method], pre-auth [required/not required], balance billing [applies/does not apply]. Written summary by [date].

tactics.name: Get a reference number first
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase. The reference number is the call's primary deliverable — without it, the carrier has no record that the verification happened.

tactics.name: Capture the four numbers
tactics.script: Use the `ask_oon_deductible_coinsurance` phrase. OON deductible YTD, OON coinsurance %, OON out-of-pocket max YTD, OON max billed cap if any. Write them down.

tactics.name: Force the methodology
tactics.script: Use the `ask_allowed_amount_methodology` phrase. Don't accept 'reasonable and customary' — push for 'Medicare x 140%' / 'UCR 80th percentile' / 'Fair Health 70th percentile' or the equivalent. Then ask for the projected allowed amount on the specific CPTs.

tactics.name: Confirm balance-billing exposure
tactics.script: Use the `ask_balance_billing_exposure` phrase. Worst case for planned OON: provider bills $10k, allowed is $3k, plan pays 60% of $3k = $1.8k, user owes $8.2k. Surface this math before the visit.

tactics.name: Check pre-auth even for OON
tactics.script: Use the `ask_preauth_requirements` phrase. Skipping OON pre-auth can drop a benefit tier or trigger denial. Get the form, deadline, and submission method.

tactics.name: Probe for a single-case agreement
tactics.when: If in-network options are limited or none for the specialty.
tactics.script: Use the `ask_single_case_agreement` phrase. SCAs can convert OON cost-shares into INN cost-shares when the expertise isn't available in-network.

tactics.name: Get it in writing
tactics.script: Use the `request_written_summary` phrase. Verbal verification is plausibly deniable; written verification with a reference number is leverage if the claim later prices differently.

boundaries: Do NOT treat verbal verification as a guarantee of payment — the call sets up dispute leverage, not a contract.
Do NOT discuss specific clinical reasons for the OON visit beyond what's needed for pre-auth.
Do NOT speculate about prior conditions or symptoms.
Do NOT commit the user to the visit on the call — the call is for information.
Do NOT agree to a 'voluntary downgrade' to in-network if the clinical need genuinely requires OON.
Do NOT share more PII than the carrier needs to look up benefits.
success_signals: All four cost-share numbers captured (deductible, coinsurance, OOP max, plus methodology).
Allowed-amount methodology disclosed with specific multiple or percentile.
Pre-auth requirements disclosed with form and deadline.
Single-case agreement path identified if applicable.
failure_signals: Rep refuses to provide methodology beyond 'reasonable and customary'.
Rep refuses to estimate allowed amount on specific CPTs.
Rep won't issue a reference number.
Rep won't put the verification in writing.

exit_strategy.on_success: Read back the seven artifacts (reference #, deductible YTD, coinsurance %, OOP max YTD, allowed-amount methodology, balance-billing applicability, pre-auth requirement). Confirm written-summary delivery. Email a recap to the user immediately.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Document refusals. Recommend the user (1) re-call and ask a different rep — answers vary, (2) request a supervisor, (3) submit the question in writing via the member portal so a written response is required.
follow_ups: Email a recap with the seven artifacts within 1 hour.
Coordinate with the provider's billing office: share the methodology and reference number so they can set patient-responsibility expectations correctly.
Save the written summary when it arrives.

required_user_info: Provider name, NPI, tax ID, address
Whether user has any in-network alternative (drives SCA eligibility)
